name: Claims resolver (OIDC Core §5.4 scope-to-claim mapping)
outcome: The library owns the OIDC Core §5.4 scope-to-claim mapping exactly, with a filter that reduces consumer-supplied claims to those the granted scopes authorize.
scope: Add the OIDC scope-to-claim mapping per OIDC Core §5.4 (exact membership) plus a scope-gated filter. No endpoint wiring. OAuthException additions are NOT in this issue — they moved to issue 1 to avoid the issue 4/6 merge conflict on OAuthException.
acceptance_criteria: The mapping matches OIDC Core §5.4 exactly: profile → name, family_name, given_name, middle_name, nickname, preferred_username, profile, picture, website, gender, birthdate, zoneinfo, locale, updated_at; email → email, email_verified; address → address (structured object: formatted, street_address, locality, region, postal_code, country); phone → phone_number, phone_number_verified. Commonly-missed members (updated_at, email_verified, phone_number_verified, structured address) are present
The filter returns only claims authorized by the granted scopes, with sub always present even when no scope-derived claims are authorized
openid + email yields { sub, email, email_verified } only — name, etc., are dropped
Unknown scopes are ignored silently (no throw); a consumer claim not authorized by any granted scope is dropped
steps: src/oidc — add the OIDC Core §5.4 scope-to-claim mapping as an exported constant; include every claim listed in §5.4 verbatim (the commonly-missed members listed above)

src/oidc — add a filter(claims, grantedScopes) function that returns the subset of claims authorized by the granted scopes, always retaining sub. address is preserved as a structured object, not flattened

test/unit — table-driven tests across scope combinations including: openid alone; openid+email; openid+address (preserves structured address); openid+all; unknown scope ignored; consumer-supplied claim not authorized by any granted scope dropped
notes: Scope-to-claim mapping is spec-defined, so the library owns it rather than the consumer. The consumer's getUserClaims returns whatever attributes it has; this filter enforces what the granted scopes permit. Depends only on issue 1 — it touches neither the oidc config block nor anything from issue 2, so it can run in parallel with the 3→4 chain. OAuthException additions are in issue 1.
